title: Square Box Frame Coffee Table
summary: An industrial-look coffee table built as a plain 2x2 box frame sitting directly on the floor, with a glued-and-screwed 1x12 slab top — no separate legs, just a low plinth.
description:
This table's base is a genuine box frame — 2x2 legs and horizontals forming two side frames, tied together by cross supports — rather than four corner legs with an apron, giving it a low, industrial plinth look. **Measure the actual glued-up width of your three 1x12 tabletop boards before cutting the cross supports** — they need to land 3" narrower than that width, and board width varies enough between boards that the plan's stated number is a starting point, not a guarantee.

Build both side frames first with the longer screws and glue, then cut and attach the five remaining cross supports to tie the two frames together, checking that the whole base sits flat on a level surface while the glue dries. **Leave the top edges of the frame raw wood, even if you're painting or staining everything else** — this is what lets the tabletop actually bond to the frame with glue rather than gluing wood to a painted, non-porous surface.

This design also scales up into an oversized version at a larger square footprint, following the same box-frame method with longer cuts throughout.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut 3" shorter than the actual combined width of the three glued-up 1x12s.

steps.title: Prepare the tabletop
steps.body: Cut and lay out the three 1x12 tabletop boards, measuring their combined width. Adjust the cross support cuts if it doesn't match the diagram.

steps.title: Build the side frames
steps.body: Build two side frames from the legs and horizontals, using the longer screws and glue.

steps.title: Join the frames with cross supports
steps.body: Cut the remaining cross supports to fit the actual tabletop width and attach to the two side frames. Set on a level surface and let the glue dry.

steps.title: Finish the frame
steps.body: Fill screw holes with wood filler, sand well, and paint or stain the frame, leaving the top edges raw wood so the tabletop can bond to them.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Attach the tabletop
steps.body: Apply glue generously to the top of the frame, place the tabletop on top, and attach with the shorter screws.

images.alt: Square box frame industrial coffee table
